response_text:
# Vimian Group (VIMIAN.ST) — Research Note

**Thesis.** Veterinary/animal-health roll-up with a heavy insider buy cluster — ~129M SEK net over 90d, led by Fidelio Vet Holding (~62M SEK across two tranches) plus a broad management group. Stock is +17.6% over 3m but still -25.7% over 12m and -31% off its 52w high, so the insider conviction is landing on a beaten-down name with room to re-rate. Horizon: weeks-to-months, with a July 29 earnings catalyst ~34 days out.

**Positives.**
- Large, concentrated insider blitz including a major holder (Fidelio) and the CEO buying — strongest signal class on Swedish names.
- Healthy revenue growth (+11.7% YoY, 13.6% 3y CAGR) and high gross margin (69%) — durable compounder profile.
- Price is well below its 52w high but holding above the 200ma (+3.4%), suggesting a base may be forming.

**Concerns.**
- **FCF is deeply negative (-243 MEUR, -16.9% yield)** with net debt of 2,827 MEUR — this is a leveraged acquisitive model that burns cash; not self-funding.
- P/E of 3.6 looks too good to be true and likely reflects one-off/accounting distortion given the cash-flow picture — I cannot reconcile a 3.6x P/E with 8.5% net margin and negative FCF from this data alone.
- Note the 2026-06-16 pattern: several managers SELL then BUY similar amounts same/next day — looks like program/mechanical reshuffling, which dilutes the conviction read on the management portion. Fidelio's buys are the clean signal.
- No news catalyst in last 30d to explain the move beyond insider flow.

**Insider / news read.** Insider signal is the whole story here and it is genuine on the Fidelio side (~62M SEK, a real holder adding). The same-day buy/sell offsets among individual managers should be discounted. No material news in 30d.

**Price read.** +17.6% 3m off a depressed base, above the 200ma but 31% below 52w high. Momentum turning up on insider support; not extended.

**Verdict.** Real insider edge on a discounted quality compounder, but the negative FCF + leverage caps conviction — *BUY — starter*.
